header: 4REVES: From Path-Level Credit to Recovery-Level Supervision

images.caption: Figure 3:Mapping Eq. (1) onto the figure.(a)Multi-turn RL training rollouts under the currentπθ\pi_{\theta}.(b)The horizontal hatched chain is a retained sequential-revision trajectory; it determines the visit-count weightρθ​(z)\rho_{\theta}(z)at each intermediate statez=(x,yt−1,ft−1)z=(x,y_{t-1},f_{t-1}). The branches at each visitedzzare fresh samples fromπθ(⋅∣z)\pi_{\theta}(\cdot\mid z); their verifier outcomes provide the per-state estimate ofVπ​(z)=𝔼y′∼πθ(⋅∣z)​[r⋆​(x,y′)]V_{\pi}(z)=\mathbb{E}_{y^{\prime}\sim\pi_{\theta}(\cdot\mid z)}[r^{\star}(x,y^{\prime})].

Figure 4:Overview of the proposed RL-based data augmentation framework. In Stage I, the current policy performs sequential revision; only trajectories that succeed within budgetKKare retained, and their intermediate states yield revision and verification prompts that are merged with the original RL prompt set. In Stage II, the policy is updated by single-turn RL on the augmented set, and the new policy regenerates the augmentation in the next epoch.

Figure 5:Out-of-distribution puzzle benchmarks (n_queens and mini_sudoku) under verifier-based stopping. REVES generalizes beyond standard reasoning benchmarks despite using checkpoints trained only on math and code.

Table 5:Effect of revision and verification prompts on AIME25.Left:1-shot (OneShot), O-4 (Oracle-4), and SC-4 (SelfConf-4) accuracy across the four configurations:RL(no augmentation),VerificationOnly(verification prompts only),RevisionOnly(revision prompts only), andREVES(both, our method).Right:TailConfidencedistributions for RevisionOnly and REVES; REVES produces sharper, better-separated correct/incorrect distributions, indicating improved confidence calibration.
Figure 6:Ablations on data augmentation (AIME24).Left:effect of augmentation budgetKK(the maximum trajectory length allowed during augmentation);right:continual versus one-shot augmentation.
Figure 7:Ablations on data augmentation (MATH500).Left:effect of augmentation frequency (continual versus one-shot);right:effect of allowed revision budgetKK.
